description: Weighted sampling matrix for the Aegis synthetic case generator. PM picked weighted random sampling on 2026-05-27. Weights sum to 1.0 per axis. Edit weights to bias the dataset distribution; the ScenarioPlanner draws one cell per case.
scope_note: MVP Part A: commercial plans only, 3 insurers, 2 denial types. Do not add Medicare/Medicaid. Do not add anything in eval/banned_topics.json.

patient_age_band.constraint_note: Commercial only — patient must not be on Medicare. Mention employer-sponsored coverage explicitly.

sub_tactic_medical_necessity.definition: Insurer says patient must try cheaper alternative X first.
Insurer demands documented conservative treatment (e.g., 6 mo PT, 6 mo diet) first.
Visit cadence requested exceeds insurer's guideline (e.g., PT > 20 visits).
Insurer claims a lower level of care suffices (IOP→outpatient, inpatient→IOP).
Insurer cites lack of RCT evidence or non-coverage for indication.
Treatment course length exceeds guideline cap.
Insurer cites the wrong MCG/InterQual/UM criterion or applies it incorrectly.

sub_tactic_prior_authorization.definition: Auth denied because peer-to-peer review was not requested in window.
Drug placed at non-preferred tier; lower-tier alternative demanded.
Out-of-network provider used without gap exception.
Continuing therapy denied for missing re-auth (e.g., Humira refill).
ER service denied because no prior auth (despite emergency exemption).
Insurer demands a cheaper modality (HSAT vs in-lab sleep study; oral vs infusion).
Annual visit cap (PT, chiro, mental health) exceeded.

joint_constraints: If patient_age_band == 71+, the case MUST include text confirming employer-sponsored commercial plan (e.g., 'still actively employed at <employer-archetype>'). Medicare-eligible-but-on-commercial is the only valid combo.
If specialty == oncology AND sub_tactic == not_evidence_based, generator MUST NOT invent a drug that does not exist on FDA labels.
Specialty 'behavioral_health' must remain bounded by docs/safety: no suicide, no self-harm; OCD/anxiety/depression/AUD are acceptable.
Specialty 'womens_health' must not include gender-affirming care for minors (banned topic).
